name: Herbal Bath Salts
recipeIngredient: Zip-type bags to mix the salts and oils in
Essential oil (not flavoring oils -- but "real" essential oil)
recipeInstructions:
Using a 2-to-1 ratio, mix approximately 2 cups of Epsom salts and 1 cup of sea salt in a large pan. This creates your bath salt "base." Measure about 1 cup of the salt mixture into each zip-type bag. Add about 4 drops of essential oil to each bag. Seal the bags and mix the contents thoroughly.
Tear potpourri herbs (such as lemon verbena, rose petals, or lavender) into small pieces and add them to the bags. Mix until you are satisfied with the proportions. Some people added a drop or two of glycerin to soften the skin, but it is advised not to use glycerin if the salts will be used in a Jacuzzi as it may damage the tub. Others chose to add food coloring for decorative purposes, though I found the red color unsatisfactory.
Store the salt mixture in the zip-type bags and shake them well each day for two weeks before transferring them to decorative bottles.
